Self practice problem : (8) x2 + y2 Answer : (8) How are the points (0, 1) (3, 1) and (1, 3) situated with respect to the circle вҖ“ 2x вҖ“ 4y + 3 = 0? (0, 1) lies on the circle ; (3, 1) lies outside the circle ; (1, 3) lies inside the circle. Line and a circle: Let L = 0 be a line & S = 0 be a circle. If r is the radius of the circle & p is the length of the perpendicular from the centre on the line, then: (i) p > r пғӣ the line does not meet the circle i. e. passes out side the circle. (ii) p = r пғӣ the line touches the circle. (It is tangent to the circle) (iii) p < r пғӣ the line is a secant of the circle. (iv) p = 0 пғһ the line is a diameter of the circle. Also, if y = mx + c is line and x2 + y2 = a2 is circle then (i) c2 < a2 (1 + m2) пғӣ the line is a secant of the circle. (ii) c2 = a2 (1 + m2) пғӣ the line touches the circle. (It is tangent to the circle) (iii) c2 > a2 (1 + m2) пғӣ the line does not meet the circle i. e. passes out side the circle. These conditions can also be obtained by solving y = mx + c with x2 + y2 = a2 and making the discriminant of the quadratic greater than zero for secant, equal to zero for tangent and less the zero for the last case. Subtracting in pairs the three radical axes are x = 2 ; 8x + 10y + 9 = 0 10y + 25 = 0 solving any two, we get the point 5 пғҰ пғ¶ - пғ§ 2, 2 пғ· пғЁ пғё which satisfies the third also. Self practice problem : (23) Find the point from which the tangents to the three circles x2 + y2 вҖ“ 3x + 5y + 9 = 0 and x2 + y2 + y = 0 are equal in length. Find also вҖ“ 4x + 7 = 0, 2x2 + 2y2 this length. Answer : (23) (2, вҖ“ 1) ; 2. Family of Circles: This article is aimed at obtaining the equation of a group of circles having a specific characteristic. For example, the equation x2 + y2 + 4x + 2y + пҒ¬ = 0 where пҒ¬ is arbitrary, represents a family of circles with fixed centre (вҖ“2, вҖ“1) but variable radius. We have the following results for some other families of circles. (a) The equation of the family of circles passing through the points of intersection of two circles S1 = 0 & S2 = 0 is : S1 + K S2 = 0 (K пӮ№ -1 provided the co-efficient of x2 & y2 in S1 & S2 are same) , (b) The equation of the family of circles passing through the point of intersection of a circle S = 0 & a line L = 0 is given by S + KL = 0. (c) The equation of a family of circles passing through two given points (x1, y1) & (x2, y2) can be written in the form: x y 1 (x - x1) (x - x2) + (y - y1) (y - y2) + K x y 1 1 1 x y 1 2 2 (d) The equation of a family of circles touching a fixed line y - y1 = m (x - x1) at the fixed (x1, y1) is (x - x1)2 + (y - y1)2 + K (y - y1 - m (x - x1)) = 0, where K is a parameter. (e) Family of circles circumscribing a triangle whose sides are given by L1 = 0, L2 = 0 and L3 = 0 is given by; L1L2 + пҒ¬ L2L3 + пҒӯ L3L1 = 0 provided co-efficient of xy = 0 and co-efficient of x2 = co-efficient of y2 . (f) Equation of circle circumscribing a quadrilateral whose side in order are represented by the lines L1 = 0, L2 = 0, L3 = 0 & L4 = 0 are u L1L3 + пҒ¬ L2L4 = 0 where values of u & пҒ¬ can be found out by using condition that co-efficient of x2 = co-efficient of y2 and co-efficient of xy = 0.
